bodyHTML:

Arc Flash Study

CareLabs is a low-cost provider of electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services for a wide range of businesses. Arc flash hazards, worker training, documentation, and PPE (Personal Protection Equipment) requirements will be checked by certified professionals to ensure your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and thorough. CareLabs may discover arc flash risks early on and take the right procedures to mitigate the impact of harmonic distortions to ensure the safety of the equipment. 

Enquire
Contact Us

ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) software is used for Arc flash analysis and research. An arc flash is the amount of light and heat produced by an arc fault. This happens when current flows via an air gap between two conductors. The arc flash ionizes the air and emits a large amount of light and heat (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). 

Arc flash analysis is the process of investigating and evaluating a facility’s electrical installations to estimate the amount of incident energy available for each piece of electrical equipment. Arc flash studies improve safe working conditions by defining safety requirements for trained electricians to operate on equipment and circuits. A short circuit and coordination analysis, and improved electrical one-line schematics and equipment placements are also provided. 

Arc Flash Research Goals:
  • To assess the amount of injury caused by incident energy emitted during an arc flash fault.
  • Changing the settings of protective equipment to decrease incident energy exposure
  • Make arc flash safety recommendations.
  • Follow your country’s standards and legislation.
  • Determine the type of PPE utilized while working.
The Negative Effects of Arc Flash:
  • Arcs produce severe burns to the skin, face, and eyes.
  • Inhaling gases and hot particles can cause catastrophic internal burns to the throat and lungs.
  • Injuries from flying debris and unfastened components

Arc flash studies are crucial for reducing hazards, increasing safety for electrical staff, and the safety of the company. Arc flash studies assist in determining electrical hazard levels and implementing appropriate safety measures to reduce the risk of burns and accidents. Arc flash analysis is critical for all utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial companies that use critical electrical components such as electrical disconnects, motor control, panel boards, and switchboards. 

The Benefits of Arc Flash Research:
  • Increased human and machine safety
  • Medical and legal expenses are reduced.
  • Regulatory compliance with safety regulations
  • Reduced standards violations and penalties

The following steps are used for arc flash analysis: 

  • Obtaining Information
  • Field verification
  • System design
  • Examining arc flashes
CareLabs Work Process

CareLabs supplies excellent services and will provide any services necessary by the site crew, local contractor, or engineering firm.

Our work method includes of the following steps:

  • Obtaining Information
  • ETAP is used to model a power system.
  • Short-circuit investigation
  • Research on the coordination of overcurrent protective devices
  • A look into arc flashes
  • Arc flash testing
  • Keeping records
  • Methods for lowering the danger of arc flash
  • Full-size one-line diagrams
  • Arc flash labelling is being installed.
  • The finished report
The Advantages of Using CareLabs Services:
  • Create more secure power systems that adhere to regulations and rules.
  • Complete integrated short circuit, overcurrent coordination, device evaluation, and arc flash evaluation modules are integrated with protective devices and bus ratings.
  • Creating a safer working environment
  • Quick and easy alternative design proposal
  • Increased safety margins using user-defined arc fault limits
  • With our computer-aided analysis, you may save time by automatically producing arc flash labels.
  • Reduce lost productivity and maintenance costs.

Crew members are assigned to different places throughout CareLabs, ensuring that our specialists are close in the event of a routine or emergency. CareLabs has quickly proven itself as an ISO 9001:2008 recognized organization, with a respected client base that has supplied honest feedback. We offer arc flash investigation and analysis services in all major cities, including Oslo, Bergen, Trondheim, and Stavanger. Contact us right now for a free arc flash research and analysis quote.

title: Harmonic Study and Analysis
bodyHTML:

  • About Us
  • Our Services

    • Harmonic Study and Analysis

    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Harmonic Study and Analysis

Electrical installation services provided by CareLabs include electrical safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification, and thorough harmonic research and analysis. Trained professionals will evaluate harmonic threats, training programs, documentation, and personal protective equipment (PPE) to verify that your electrical safety program is up to date, compliant, and complete.

Enquire
Contact Us

CareLabs can detect harmonics early on and take appropriate steps to mitigate the impact of harmonic distortions to ensure the equipment’s safety.  Harmonic voltages and currents in the electrical system can generate inefficiencies. They produce wasteful heat and have the potential to decrease power quality. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical technique for estimating potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels. Based on data, harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical method for estimating potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels. 

Harmonic frequencies are introduced into the electrical system by nonlinear electrical switching equipment such as AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, computers, and so on. There is a substantial risk of equipment malfunctioning with high harmonic voltage/current levels. As a result, prompt harmonic analysis and study are vital. Harmonic limitations are currently set at 5% in 415 voltage systems, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics. 

Effects of Harmonic Distortion:
  • It circulates current and high voltage.
  • Excessive voltage distortion causes equipment failure and damage.
  • The existence of internal energy in components increases, resulting in part failure and a shorter life duration.
  • Branch circuit breaker trip owing to a fault
  • Error measurement
  • Explosions and fires in the wiring and distribution system
  • Outages caused by generators
  • A reduction in the system power factor
  • Installation and utility costs are exorbitant.
  • Productivity declines
  • Downtime and unneeded road trips
  • The utilization of larger motors

Harmonic difficulties are highly unusual to arise, but when they do, they have a negative impact on power system reliability, power quality, and efficiency. As a result, harmonic research and analysis are critical. 

  • It safeguards against voltage and current harmonics.
  • Limitation on frequency fluctuation
  • Voltage and current imbalance
  • Enhance power factor analysis and keep the value close to one.
  • Voltage fluctuations are regulated.

Harmonic research and analysis will supply insight into the potential causes and effects of power quality concerns, helping in the avoidance of harmonic-related issues during the design stage and lowering the risk of unintended consequences. 

Harmonic Analysis and Study Work Process at CareLabs:
  • Highlight accessible nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and medium voltage cables of lengthy length on the electrical one-line diagram.
  • Decide the point of common connection (PCC)
  • Find which in-plant system buses are prone to harmonic distortions.
  • Gather harmonics information from all nonlinear loads.
  • Obtain crucial data on voltage and current harmonics at the selected PCC, including the maximum and lowest short circuit fault levels.
  • Conduct harmonic analysis on the electrical network using ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program).
  • Calculate the total and discrete levels of current and voltage wave distortions at the buses and the PCC.
  • Make a graph of the harmonic frequency spectrum.
  • If the levels of harmonic distortion surpass the permitted thresholds, a suitable mitigation strategy is proposed and applied.
  • After implementing the mitigation solution, rerun the analysis.
Advantages of CareLabs Service:

Stage 2 entails higher ratings if the background harmonics have already been measured.

  • Stage 2 allows for changing converter size based on fault level and converter type if the background is less than 25%.
  • Converter ratings of half the amount mentioned above are permissible if the backdrop is between 25% and 75%.
  • For higher background, a comprehensive harmonic study is carried out.

CareLabs has crew members stationed in various areas to ensure our specialists are close by in case of a routine or emergency. CareLabs has quickly established itself as an ISO 9001:2008 recognized company with a strong client base that has supplied positive feedback. To name a few major Norwegian towns, we offer harmonic research and analysis services in Oslo, Bergen, Trondheim, and Stavanger. Get in touch with us at once to schedule your harmonic research and analysis in Norway or to request a price!

title: Motor Start Analysis
bodyHTML:
• Home
  • About Us
  • Our Services
    • Arc Flash Study
    • Harmonic Study and Analysis
    • Power Quality Analysis
    • Power System Study and Analysis
    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Motor Start Analysis

CareLabs provides Motor start study and analysis services to businesses around Norway. Our motor start analysis service’s main purpose is to discover out what weird things happen when a large motor is turned on. Other than ensuring that the electric motor is correctly connected to the line voltage, there are other factors to consider before starting an electric motor. Nuisance tripping, high nominal currents, and flickering or fading lights, for example, are all indications that a power system isn’t performing properly.

Enquire
Contact Us

CareLabs will send experienced specialists to your site to gather both fresh and old information concerning the installation of your motors. Then, using the data and information gathered from the manufacturer, calculations are performed to forecast what will happen in the future and devise solutions to mitigate the impacts.

Static and dynamic simulation can be used to investigate motor starting methods. We primarily investigate dynamic motor starting studies, which attempt to imitate motor starting conditions such as:

  • The measured starting impedance of the electric motor is utilized to model it in order to obtain the load flow solution.
  • The short-circuit approach is used to calculate the current in pre-fault short circuit circumstances, while the voltage drop method is used to calculate the current in a motor when it is initially turned on.
  • When the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics are precisely modelled, the dynamic model of the electric motor may be employed in a conventional transient stability procedure to determine the effect of motor starting.
  • Transient stability simulations may be used when a thorough dynamic model of the electric motor is not available.
Norway Should Conduct a Motor Start Analysis:

Motors are becoming increasingly common in modern industrial systems, and the size of electric motors is increasing at an exponential rate. If you switch on a large motor that is linked to the power line, it might cause serious difficulties for the electric motor, any adjacent loads, even buses that are located far away from the location of the motor. The voltage at the motor terminal must be at least 80% of the rated voltage or greater for an electric motor to start. Voltage difference has a direct influence on the load torque characteristic of the motor since the square of a motor’s terminal voltage is precisely proportional to its load torque.  

If the voltage drop is significant, the strain on the operating machines may be greater than their “breakdown torque,” causing them to slow down or even halt. Other types of loads, such as electrical equipment, sensitive control devices, lighting loads, and so on, are also affected by the voltage drop. Furthermore, excessive beginning current reduces terminal voltage, causing all systems that service a steady load to require additional current. Low voltage relays have also been discovered to be broken. 

When Is a Motor Start Analysis Necessary?
  • Before or during the purchase of a large motor.
  • If there is no generator and the motor rating is greater than 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
  • If the generator is the system’s sole source of power and the motor rating exceeds 10-15% of the generator’s KVA rating.
  • A motor start study may be required if a large number of motors start at the same time for a process in an industry.
  • Because smaller power systems often have less power, voltage drop issues are exacerbated when starting a high-power motor. Looking at how motors start might help you understand the output of tiny systems with generators.
Motor Start Pros and Cons Analysis:

Investigate how a motor starts up. Select a motor or method of starting it that has the optimal speed-torque characteristics for the project. To avoid exceeding the torque limitations of the equipment, particular loads must be accelerated in a controlled manner. You may examine how the shaft of the motor functions at any moment by doing a motor start examination. As a result, it aids in determining how much torque the equipment can withstand.

Motor starting studies are required to determine how beginning affects the voltage of the machine and if an electric motor can start and get up to speed quickly.  CareLabs provides motor start studies performed by trained specialists to assist you in determining how well your electric motor is performing.  

We provide motor start analysis services in Oslo, Bergen, Trondheim, and Stavanger, among other significant cities. Please contact us as soon as possible to book your motor starting study or to obtain a price.

title: Power Quality Analysis
bodyHTML:
• Home
  • About Us
  • Our Services
    • Arc Flash Study
    • Harmonic Study and Analysis
    • Power Quality Analysis
    • Power System Study and Analysis
    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Power Quality Analysis

CareLabs inspects the quality of power for companies in Norway. Our aim is to protect your electrical systems, appliances, and equipment from breaking down due to variations in power supply. 

Enquire
Contact Us

Many organizations and an increasing number of individual consumers increasingly employ power-sensitive data gathering, monitoring, and instrumentation devices. Most electrical appliances are designed to manage specific supply variations, but relatively few are designed to handle the wide variety of voltage fluctuations that occur with distributed electricity. CareLabs offers experts that can assist you with power quality issues such as equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning. 

The Purpose of Power Quality Analysis Is as Follows:
  • The procedure for determining what is wrong with the electrical system and load.
  • Examining the work environment in order to improve simulation approaches for locating solutions to power quality challenges.
  • Determining how and how well load equipment will function
  • Improve the machine’s power factor and overall performance.
  • Avoid using too much energy.
  • To keep things from breaking down and coming to a halt.
  • Getting rid of harmonics, spikes, and transients that degrade power quality.
  • Assist in preventing voltage/frequency spikes and PLCs from becoming stuck.
  • To avoid transformers overheating, capacitors bursting, and unpleasant trips.
  • Reduce your utility expenses significantly.
  • Increase the lifespan of the electrical network and its components.
  • Examine electrical equipment to ensure it can withstand modifications.
Norway Should Conduct a Power Quality Analysis:

Power outages occur on all electrical networks, and they are more often when storms, natural catastrophes, or accidents destroy power lines. Power supply changes are usual. 

Changes, disruptions, and distortions in the delivery of power from an electricity network are classified into three types:

1.A frequency deviation occurs when a frequency deviates from its native frequency range.

2.Voltage disruptions occur when the voltage’s form remains constant, but its amplitude exceeds the typical range.

2.1 Voltage sags (dips) and voltage spikes lasting up to one minute are examples of short-term variations.

2.2 Under voltages and over voltages are examples of longer-lasting variations.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage: This occurs when the load is not distributed equally across the phases, causing the equipment to overheat.

2.4 The voltage differential between ground and neutral

3.Disturbances induced by distortion, which occur when the voltage’s shape is not smooth or consistent.

3.1 Harmonics and inter-harmonics cause electronics to overheat, lose power, use power inefficiently, and have resonance issues.

3.2 Transients that last less than a tenth of a second can cause switch contact surfaces to be damaged, the system to be less efficient, and motor drives and control to fail prematurely.

What Is Power Quality Analysis?

Different procedures must be used to identify various power quality issues. Among these approaches are:

  • Harmonic Analysis: Computer algorithms and reduction techniques are used in harmonic analysis to discover and anticipate potential harmonic difficulties.
  • Surge and Transient Analysis: Surges and transients will be discovered in this investigation.
  • Voltage Dips and Climbs Analysis: This analysis would monitor the voltage’s short-term drops and rises, as well as its value and course.
  • What a Reactive Power Works: The purpose of this study is to figure out the best reactive power at the distribution and load ends.
  • Analysis of Captive Electricity: This study would assess and coordinate captive power to satisfy demand while minimizing energy surcharges and fuel use.
  • Load Flow Analysis: Based on the operational circumstances, this analysis forecasts power flow magnitudes, power factor, voltage levels, and losses in system branches.

CareLabs supplies the finest service possible to its clients to improve the quality and dependability of their electrical systems. We provide power quality analysis services in Oslo, Bergen, Trondheim, and Stavanger, among other significant cities. Contact us today to book a power quality study or to get a quotation.

bodyText:
Home
About Us
Our Services
Contact Us
Power Quality Analysis

CareLabs inspects the quality of power for companies in Norway. Our aim is to protect your electrical systems, appliances, and equipment from breaking down due to variations in power supply. 

Enquire
Contact Us

Many organizations and an increasing number of individual consumers increasingly employ power-sensitive data gathering, monitoring, and instrumentation devices. Most electrical appliances are designed to manage specific supply variations, but relatively few are designed to handle the wide variety of voltage fluctuations that occur with distributed electricity. CareLabs offers experts that can assist you with power quality issues such as equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning. 

The Purpose of Power Quality Analysis Is as Follows:
The procedure for determining what is wrong with the electrical system and load.
Examining the work environment in order to improve simulation approaches for locating solutions to power quality challenges.
Determining how and how well load equipment will function
Improve the machine’s power factor and overall performance.
Avoid using too much energy.
To keep things from breaking down and coming to a halt.
Getting rid of harmonics, spikes, and transients that degrade power quality.
Assist in preventing voltage/frequency spikes and PLCs from becoming stuck.
To avoid transformers overheating, capacitors bursting, and unpleasant trips.
Reduce your utility expenses significantly.
Increase the lifespan of the electrical network and its components.
Examine electrical equipment to ensure it can withstand modifications.
Norway Should Conduct a Power Quality Analysis:

Power outages occur on all electrical networks, and they are more often when storms, natural catastrophes, or accidents destroy power lines. Power supply changes are usual. 

Changes, disruptions, and distortions in the delivery of power from an electricity network are classified into three types:

1.A frequency deviation occurs when a frequency deviates from its native frequency range.

2.Voltage disruptions occur when the voltage’s form remains constant, but its amplitude exceeds the typical range.

2.1 Voltage sags (dips) and voltage spikes lasting up to one minute are examples of short-term variations.

2.2 Under voltages and over voltages are examples of longer-lasting variations.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage: This occurs when the load is not distributed equally across the phases, causing the equipment to overheat.

2.4 The voltage differential between ground and neutral

3.Disturbances induced by distortion, which occur when the voltage’s shape is not smooth or consistent.

3.1 Harmonics and inter-harmonics cause electronics to overheat, lose power, use power inefficiently, and have resonance issues.

3.2 Transients that last less than a tenth of a second can cause switch contact surfaces to be damaged, the system to be less efficient, and motor drives and control to fail prematurely.

What Is Power Quality Analysis?

Different procedures must be used to identify various power quality issues. Among these approaches are:

Harmonic Analysis: Computer algorithms and reduction techniques are used in harmonic analysis to discover and anticipate potential harmonic difficulties.
Surge and Transient Analysis: Surges and transients will be discovered in this investigation.
Voltage Dips and Climbs Analysis: This analysis would monitor the voltage’s short-term drops and rises, as well as its value and course.
What a Reactive Power Works: The purpose of this study is to figure out the best reactive power at the distribution and load ends.
Analysis of Captive Electricity: This study would assess and coordinate captive power to satisfy demand while minimizing energy surcharges and fuel use.
Load Flow Analysis: Based on the operational circumstances, this analysis forecasts power flow magnitudes, power factor, voltage levels, and losses in system branches.

CareLabs supplies the finest service possible to its clients to improve the quality and dependability of their electrical systems. We provide power quality analysis services in Oslo, Bergen, Trondheim, and Stavanger, among other significant cities. Contact us today to book a power quality study or to get a quotation.

title: Power System Study and Analysis
bodyHTML:
• Home
  • About Us
  • Our Services
    • Arc Flash Study
    • Harmonic Study and Analysis
    • Power Quality Analysis
    • Power System Study and Analysis
    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Power System Study and Analysis

CareLabs provides electrical installation services such as electrical safety inspections, simulations of electrical system design, power quality analysis studies, and power system research and analysis. 

Enquire
Contact Us

ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program), the premier tool for power system transient analysis, is used by our team of electrical specialists to conduct load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies. It enables us to collaborate directly with our clients to devise and supply the finest power compensation and solutions. 

What Is the Purpose of a Power System Analysis Study?

Every industrial or commercial structure needs a steady, dependable, and well-maintained power system. Even a little power loss or failure can create key issues, such as extended downtime, damage to critical equipment, and the expense of repairing it.

A power system analysis study might be performed on your building’s power system regularly to ensure it is safe, efficient, and dependable in both normal and abnormal conditions. A power system must be thoroughly analyzed at every stage of design, installation, and operation. We will investigate and analyze the power system using the ETAP software program to gain a sense of how it will perform under normal conditions and when a fault occurs rapidly. It is a collection of engineering analytic studies that examine how an electric power system responds to events across various time periods, such as transient, dynamic, and static. 

Many different studies may be used to determine the state of your facility’s electrical system. It might be included in a report on a power system analysis. Here are a couple such examples: 

  • The circuit breaker
  • Putting together a security plan
  • Load Movement
  • Continuously changing evaluations
  • Arc Flash: Investigating It
  • How well does power work?
  • Harmonics research
  • Starting up the engine
  • Earth science research

It enables organizations to create a standard for various aspects of plant operation, particularly the electrical safety program. These studies may prove that operations are safe and dependable. 

A Load Flow Analysis:

Load flow studies allow you to determine the voltages and currents in various portions of the network and select the proper rated equipment. The built-in load flow function in ETAP allows you to learn about a power system’s voltages and currents, as well as how much power is being utilized and its power factor. 

Short Circuit Research:

Short-circuit studies are required to determine how a power system functions when there is a problem. Doing short circuit investigations by hand takes a long time, and using software like ETAP that can perform the job properly is significantly faster. ETAP is used to simulate system protection devices and determine the effects of 3-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures on distribution systems. 

Transient Investigation:

The magnitude and form of overvoltage and current spikes induced by lightning strikes and switching operations are estimated in transient studies. You can determine how much insulation and surge protection you require based on the results. 

Protection Coordination Research:

This research is designed to ensure that your system’s security components are functioning properly. It checks to determine if they are the correct size and fit the item they are protecting. 

In general, the following critical phases are included in our power system analysis research process: 

  • How to Obtain Data
  • Data analysis and client meetings
  • Obtaining equipment information
  • A single-line diagram model
  • ETAP power system simulation
  • Examine the short circuit and load flow.
  • Investigate ways to coordinate protection.
  • An arc flash analysis
  • A glance at the entire research report
  • Risk assessment and risk management
  • Project management
The parts of CareLabs' power system analysis are as follows:
  • Opportunities to reduce energy use and expenditures.
  • Reports that highlight potential problems.
  • Solutions suggestions
  • Information on how and where energy is used.
  • Confirmation of protective device ratings.
  • Assist in preventing damage and injury.
  • Harmonics and transients’ effects

In our power system analysis studies, we examine the client’s electrical system and give recommendations on how to improve its performance and ensure it always has power. We examine and analyze electricity systems in major cities including Oslo, Bergen, Trondheim, and Stavanger. Contact us immediately to schedule a power system evaluation and analysis or to get a price.
